features.description: The Xcode project can be opened and built by a fresh developer or agent session.
acceptance: Run ./init.sh from the repository root.
Verify xcodebuild lists the Parrot scheme.
Verify the Debug macOS build completes with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO.
features.notes: ./init.sh completed successfully in the current environment. The harness now builds into the repo-local ./.DerivedData path to avoid multiple system DerivedData app copies during TCC-sensitive debugging. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: ./init.sh completed successfully after the SwiftUI/AppKit layout changes and Debug signing still reports designated => identifier "com.example.parrot". 2026-07-04 high-fidelity HTML pass verification: ./init.sh completed successfully after the titlebar/sidebar/form/pane refactor; ./init.sh --run also rebuilt, signed, and opened the fixed Debug app bundle.

features.description: A fresh agent can understand startup, current progress, and next work without prior chat history.
acceptance: Read parrot-progress.md for current state and next steps.
Read feature_list.json for implementation priorities and verification criteria.
Use ./init.sh for project metadata and build verification.
features.notes: Initial handoff files are present; keep them updated after feature work. AGENTS.md and parrot-progress.md document ./init.sh --run as the stable local debug entry point: stop old Parrot instances, build into ./.DerivedData, and open that exact app bundle.

features.description: The project can produce GitHub-style unsigned macOS Release packages from SemVer-tagged commits.
acceptance: Document the SemVer, Git tag, and GitHub Release asset workflow.
Provide a repeatable script that builds an unsigned Release app.
Generate both .dmg and .zip assets under Dist/.
Generate SHA256SUMS.txt and GitHub-style RELEASE_NOTES.md.
Reject formal releases when MARKETING_VERSION, the current tag, or the git worktree state is invalid.
Allow clearly labeled local dev packaging before a release tag exists.
features.notes: Added Docs/release-process.md and Scripts/package-release.sh. The release process documents SemVer, v<MARKETING_VERSION> tags, GitHub Release assets, Gatekeeper warnings, checksum verification, and no-retag safety rules. The packaging script reads MARKETING_VERSION, CURRENT_PROJECT_VERSION, and PRODUCT_BUNDLE_IDENTIFIER from Config/Release.xcconfig, enforces SemVer, rejects formal releases from dirty worktrees or non-matching tags, supports local --allow-untagged dev packages, builds Release with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO, verifies app bundle identifier/version/build metadata, ad-hoc signs the full app bundle for local resource-seal integrity with a stable local designated requirement based on the bundle identifier, verifies codesign --deep --strict before packaging, rejects pure cdhash designated requirements so Screen Recording TCC grants are not tied to a per-build hash, detects architecture, and creates .dmg, .zip, SHA256SUMS.txt, and RELEASE_NOTES.md under Dist/. Follow-up fix for v0.1.2-alpha Gatekeeper 'app is damaged' dialog: the previous package had only executable-level ad-hoc/linker signing and no sealed resources, causing codesign verification to fail with 'code has no resources but signature indicates they must be present'. Follow-up fix for v0.1.3-alpha Screen Recording permission loop: the previous pure ad-hoc release had designated requirement cdhash-only, which produced duplicate Parrot.app entries in System Settings and allowed users to enable an old hash while the current app remained unauthorized. Verified bash -n Scripts/package-release.sh, git diff --check, full Scripts/package-release.sh --allow-untagged Release build, shasum -a 256 -c SHA256SUMS.txt for the generated dmg and zip, mounted the generated DMG and verified Parrot.app with codesign --verify --deep --strict --verbose=4, confirmed the generated Release app, DMG app, ZIP app, and installed /Applications/Parrot.app all report designated => identifier "com.example.parrot", confirmed the DMG app has Sealed Resources version 2 and CodeResources, confirmed spctl still rejects only because there is no Developer ID signature, and unzipped the ZIP artifact to verify the packaged app signature. Real System Settings inspection on 2026-06-23 confirmed two Parrot.app Screen Recording entries from old cdhash identities, matching the reported restart-still-unauthorized symptom.

features.description: The harness routes UI/UX work to DESIGN.md as the normative visual source, treats /Design prototypes as a conditional future-refactor reference only, and records design verification evidence.
acceptance: DESIGN.md exists at the repository root with YAML tokens plus Markdown prose sections.
DESIGN.md is not ignored and is intended to be version controlled as the team-wide design contract.
DESIGN.md update policy is documented as stable design contract maintenance, not per-session or per-feature tuning.
DESIGN.md token/prose consistency has been reviewed; any obvious safe mismatch is fixed and any design-decision mismatch is recorded as a follow-up.
AGENTS.md has a Design Harness section defining mandatory DESIGN.md triggers for UI/UX work and conditional /Design triggers.
AGENTS.md states PRDs are conditional references only: read them only when the user explicitly requests PRD reference/alignment/review or when doing PRD/planning work.

AGENTS.md records the conflict priority: explicit user instruction > privacy/security/macOS permission/Keychain > macOS HIG > DESIGN.md > current code established shape, with PRDs and /Design excluded from day-to-day arbitration unless explicitly requested.
Design/README.md documents /Design as a future one-time-refactor reference that lags behind code and follows DESIGN.md.
Design/README.md lists the current quick_text_translation, screenshot_translation, and settings code.html prototypes.
/Design is not listed as a required input for day-to-day feature development and is only triggered by explicit user prototype/refactor reference requests.
Existing UI-class features include acceptance criteria requiring DESIGN.md review and recorded design verification evidence, without defaulting to /Design prototypes as the baseline.
A manual npx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md attempt is recorded in feature_list.json notes or parrot-progress.md, including any npm/network limitation.
Design harness setup introduces no product code, dependency, signing, bundle identifier, default build flow, privacy, or persistence changes.
AGENTS.md workflow defaults to current code, feature_list.json, parrot-progress.md, and user intent for product context instead of default PRD reads.
feature_list.json keeps PRDs in conditional_reference_documents rather than default source_documents, so PRDs are not implied default inputs.
features.notes: Design Harness landed on 2026-07-03. DESIGN.md is the mandatory UI/UX design source and /Design is conditional-only for explicit prototype/refactor requests. DESIGN.md audit fixed safe token/prose mismatches by aligning prose color references to YAML tokens, adding native SwiftUI/AppKit implementation guidance, and changing body letterSpacing tokens from string 0 to 0px. Lint record: initial sandbox npx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md failed with ENOTFOUND registry.npmjs.org; approved npm-backed retry reported 2 errors for letterSpacing dimensions; after the token fix, npx @google/design.md lint DESIGN.md passed with errors=0 warnings=0 infos=1. AGENTS.md now routes UI work to DESIGN.md, keeps PRDs conditional-only, excludes both PRDs and /Design from day-to-day arbitration, and records the required conflict priority. Design/README.md declares /Design as future one-time-refactor assets and lists quick_text_translation, screenshot_translation, and settings code.html prototypes. Existing UI-class feature acceptance now requires DESIGN.md review and recorded design evidence for future UI changes; existing pass statuses remain prior product verification records. No product code, dependencies, signing, bundle identifier, default build flow, privacy, or persistence behavior changed. Follow-up adjustment on 2026-07-03: PRDs were downgraded from default product-behavior references to conditional-only references because PRDs can also lag behind current code; daily work now defaults to current code, feature_list.json, parrot-progress.md, and user intent unless PRD alignment is explicitly requested. feature_list.json now keeps PRDs under conditional_reference_documents instead of default source_documents. Follow-up on 2026-07-04: /Design now uses HTML-only prototype references; AGENTS.md, Design/README.md, parrot-progress.md, feature_list.json, and Docs/design-md-harness-integration-research.md no longer describe prototype image pairs; the three former static exports under Design/*/ were deleted.

features.description: Current Parrot UI/UX is aligned with DESIGN.md and the explicitly referenced /Design prototypes while preserving native macOS workflows and privacy constraints.
acceptance: Read AGENTS.md, parrot-progress.md, feature_list.json, DESIGN.md, Design/README.md, and the relevant /Design quick text, screenshot translation, and settings prototypes before editing UI.
Use DESIGN.md as the primary design contract and treat /Design prototypes as reference assets that can be overridden by current code, macOS HIG, privacy, and native implementation constraints.

Improve menu-bar entry, quick actions, Quick Text, Screenshot Translation, screenshot/OCR feedback, Settings, Provider configuration, shortcut configuration, translation preferences, glossary, history, empty states, error states, loading states, window sizing, and accessibility-oriented labels.
Keep API Keys in Keychain only, keep screenshots local, do not upload screenshots, do not persist screenshot images or geometry, and do not add heavy cross-platform UI frameworks.
Run a Debug build and at least one smoke test beyond compilation; record any environment limitation for real GUI shortcut or screenshot automation.
features.notes: Read AGENTS.md, parrot-progress.md, feature_list.json, DESIGN.md, Design/README.md, Design/quick_text_translation/code.html, Design/screenshot_translation/code.html, and Design/settings/code.html. Chose native macOS implementation over web-prototype details where they conflicted: system font, semantic colors, native controls, tonal panels, and 8px-or-less radii were used instead of hard-coded Inter/Tailwind styling. Implemented shared Parrot UI components, SF Symbol menu icons, refined Quick Text layout, compact language controls, screenshot result summary and comparison panes, screenshot permission error layout, Settings/Provider/Shortcuts/Translation/Privacy/Glossary polish, history empty/record/detail states, and screenshot selection overlay feedback. Privacy boundaries unchanged: API Keys remain Keychain-only, screenshots remain local, history remains text-only, and no screenshot upload or new persistence was added. Verified git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON parse, ./init.sh Debug build/signing, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke, custom-shortcuts-e2e, ocr-source-text-editing-e2e, and parrot-history-persistence-smoke. Existing translation-history-e2e still fails only at the current session NSPasteboard assertion, matching the known environment limitation. Real CGEvent shortcut smoke launched the Debug app, but this automation session returned hiservices-xpcservice warnings and did not deliver shortcuts; pgrep confirmed the Parrot Debug process and codesign -dr confirmed identifier "com.example.parrot". Second pass on 2026-07-04 explicitly re-read the full /Design code.html files and aligned the SwiftUI/AppKit structure to the HTML intent: Quick Text keeps a fixed footer action bar and more prototype-like content rhythm, language controls are a compact one-line native toolbar, Screenshot Translation uses a tighter OCR summary plus two-pane comparison workspace, and Settings now has the prototype's sidebar shell with real Quick Text/Screenshot/History shortcuts and Settings sub-section navigation. Verified git diff --check, JSON parse, ./init.sh Debug build/signing, ./init.sh --run Debug launch, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke, custom-shortcuts-e2e, keychain-cache-e2e, and ocr-source-text-editing-e2e. Adversarial smoke covered long mixed Chinese/English text, view instantiation at target window sizes, and history JSON excluding image/screenRect/base64 fields. Current automation still cannot deliver global shortcut CGEvents, returning hiservices-xpcservice connection warnings. High-fidelity HTML pass on 2026-07-04: rechecked Design/quick_text_translation/code.html, Design/screenshot_translation/code.html, and Design/settings/code.html as the visible target. Quick Text now uses an integrated 44px prototype titlebar with native traffic lights, right-side History/Settings actions, 900x640 content, 32px content margin, title-case section labels, and language controls folded into the Input/Translation headers. Screenshot Translation now uses an integrated titlebar, 1024x720 content, compact OCR summary, compact primary status pill, larger original/translation panes, and language controls embedded in the pane headers. Settings now uses the prototype's 52px titlebar, 208px sidebar, nested text-only Settings subitems, 980px width, 700px Model target height, right-aligned label-column provider form, and bottom action bar. Native macOS controls, system font, semantic colors, Keychain-only API Key storage, local-only OCR, text-only history, and no screenshot upload/persistence were preserved. Verified git diff --check, ./init.sh, ./init.sh --run, pgrep Debug app process, codesign designated requirement identifier "com.example.parrot", custom-shortcuts-e2e, keychain-cache-e2e, ocr-source-text-editing-e2e, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke.

features.description: The app runs as a macOS menu-bar utility with entries for key actions.
acceptance: Launch the app and verify a menu-bar item appears.
Open settings from the menu-bar item.
Open quick text translation from the menu-bar item.
Trigger screenshot translation from the menu-bar item.
Quit the app from the menu-bar item.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Added an AppKit NSStatusItem with Quick Text Translation, Screenshot Translation, Pause Shortcuts, Settings, and Quit Parrot menu entries. The SwiftUI app no longer opens a default WindowGroup on launch, so cold start shows the status item without an extra main window that can be confused with the status menu. Verified via Accessibility that the status menu lists all expected entries and that Quick Text Translation and Settings open their windows. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: menu items now use native SF Symbol images for Quick Text, Screenshot, History, Shortcuts, Settings, and Quit while preserving the existing status item and action routing. Verified by ./init.sh build/signing; real menu AX interaction was not repeated because current shortcut/window automation was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings.

features.description: The app supports global shortcuts for screenshot translation and quick text translation.
acceptance: Configure default shortcuts for screenshot translation and text translation.
Verify shortcuts work while another app is frontmost.
Verify shortcuts can be paused or disabled.
Surface an actionable error if required accessibility permissions are missing.
features.notes: Verified with automated GUI smoke test using Swift CGEvent and Accessibility APIs. Finder was frontmost when Cmd+Shift+T opened Quick Text Translation and Cmd+Shift+2 opened Screenshot Translation. AXPress verified Pause Shortcuts changes the menu to Resume Shortcuts and prevents both shortcuts from opening windows, then Resume Shortcuts restores both shortcuts. Debug build succeeded with CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO. Carbon HotKey registration does not require Accessibility permission for the app itself; Accessibility was only used by the test harness automation.

features.description: Users can select an arbitrary screen region for screenshot translation.
acceptance: Invoke screenshot mode from the shortcut or menu.
Drag to select a region on the active screen.
Cancel selection with Esc.
Pass the selected image region to the OCR pipeline.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Added a native AppKit full-screen overlay for region selection. Follow-up fixes made the app an LSUIElement menu-bar utility, removed the default launch WindowGroup, and changed screenshot capture to snapshot every screen before the overlay appears, then crop the selected region from that cached screenshot. Added Screen Recording preflight/request before snapshotting, because macOS can return a desktop-only image when permission is missing. A release-package regression showed CGRequestScreenCaptureAccess can present the macOS Screen Recording prompt while returning false for the current attempt; the app now suppresses the Parrot error window only for the first request-presented state, then shows Parrot's actionable permission guidance on a later attempt if Screen Recording is still not granted. Follow-up Debug workflow fix: ./init.sh now ad-hoc signs the built Debug app bundle with the same stable designated requirement as the unsigned release package, identifier "com.example.parrot", after signing Debug-only nested dylibs. This prevents ./init.sh --run builds from using per-build cdhash TCC identities that do not match /Applications/Parrot.app. Verified ./init.sh Debug build/signing, codesign --verify --deep --strict, codesign -dr - reporting identifier "com.example.parrot" for both the Debug app and installed /Applications/Parrot.app, source-linked screen-capture access gate E2E, and a real Debug app smoke where Cmd+Shift+2 opened the Screenshot Translation permission window only because this machine has not granted Screen Recording to Parrot. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: screenshot result, OCR failure, permission error, and selection overlay visual feedback were refined without changing local OCR, screenshot upload, or image persistence behavior. Verified by ./init.sh, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke, and ocr-source-text-editing-e2e; real screenshot shortcut injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings. 2026-07-04 HTML-driven second pass: Screenshot Translation result keeps editable OCR source text but compresses the OCR preview/status summary to match the prototype's compact status area, leaving more room for the original/translation panes. Screenshot selection overlay remains local-only. Verified by ./init.sh and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke; real shortcut CGEvent injection remains blocked by hiservices-xpcservice in this automation session. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity HTML pass: Screenshot Translation result now more closely follows Design/screenshot_translation/code.html with integrated 44px titlebar, right-side History/Settings actions, 1024x720 content, compact OCR summary, compact Translation ready pill, and taller original/translation panes. The selection overlay and local OCR behavior remain unchanged. Verified by ./init.sh, ./init.sh --run, ocr-source-text-editing-e2e, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke.

features.description: The app uses local OCR to recognize text from screenshots before translation.
acceptance: Recognize text from a selected screenshot region using local macOS capabilities.
Do not upload screenshot images by default.
Show a clear message when no text is detected.
Preserve paragraph structure where OCR output allows it.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Implemented local OCR with Vision VNRecognizeTextRequest. The screenshot image stays local; the pipeline returns recognized text only and shows a clear no-text message when Vision finds no candidates. Paragraph/line structure is preserved by joining recognized observations with newlines. Follow-up optimization for Chinese OCR scales small selections up to 4x before recognition, prioritizes zh-Hans/zh-Hant before en-US, and lowers minimumTextHeight for compact UI text. Verified with ./init.sh Debug build and equivalent smoke tests: English two-line image recognized "Hello Parrot OCR" and "Translate this text"; dark compact mixed Chinese/English image recognized "时间线" and "Cue-Pro". 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: screenshot result, OCR failure, permission error, and selection overlay visual feedback were refined without changing local OCR, screenshot upload, or image persistence behavior. Verified by ./init.sh, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke, and ocr-source-text-editing-e2e; real screenshot shortcut injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings. 2026-07-04 adversarial verification: ocr-source-text-editing-e2e and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke passed with long mixed Chinese/English OCR text. History persistence assertions confirmed no image, screenRect, or base64 fields are written.

features.description: Users can configure an OpenAI-compatible LLM provider.
acceptance: Enter Base URL, model name, and API Key.
Test the provider connection.
Show concise errors for invalid credentials, network failure, and timeout.
Avoid hard-coded provider-specific assumptions beyond OpenAI-compatible defaults.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Implemented ProviderSettingsView for provider preset, Base URL, model, and API Key entry. Default recommended preset is DeepSeek with https://api.deepseek.com and deepseek-v4-flash, based on https://api-docs.deepseek.com/zh-cn/. Additional presets include GLM with https://open.bigmodel.cn/api/paas/v4 and glm-4.7, OpenAI, and Custom. Non-secret settings are stored in UserDefaults; API Keys are saved/replaced/deleted via macOS Keychain per provider. Settings now shows an API Key setup guide when the current provider has no non-secret setup record, and the app opens Settings on startup when setup is required. Connection testing uses a freshly entered API Key directly after saving, or performs a non-interactive saved-key read that reports an in-app re-entry error instead of showing a system Keychain password prompt. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, source-linked keychain UX E2E, and a real Debug app smoke where missing setup opens Settings. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: DESIGN.md and explicit /Design prototypes were reviewed; the changed surface now uses shared native header/status/panel/footer components, semantic macOS colors, system font, native controls, and 8px-or-less rounded panels. Verified by ./init.sh Debug build/signing plus /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke; related source E2Es passed where applicable. Real CGEvent shortcut smoke was limited by hiservices-xpcservice event-delivery warnings in this automation session. 2026-07-04 HTML-driven second pass: Settings now follows the /Design HTML sidebar shell while preserving native SwiftUI/AppKit controls. Provider configuration remains in Model, with Keychain-only API Key handling unchanged. Verified by ./init.sh, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke, and keychain-cache-e2e. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity HTML pass: Settings/Model now follows Design/settings/code.html more closely with a 52px titlebar, 208px left sidebar, text-only nested Model/Shortcuts/Translation/Privacy subitems, 980px shell width, 700px Model target height, right-aligned label-column provider form, and bottom Save/Test/Delete action bar. Provider presets, Base URL, model, Keychain API Key save/test/delete, and non-interactive Keychain behavior are unchanged. Verified by ./init.sh, ./init.sh --run, keychain-cache-e2e, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke.

features.description: API keys are stored in macOS Keychain and never written to repository files or logs.
acceptance: Save API Key to Keychain.
Read API Key from Keychain for requests.
Delete or replace API Key from settings.
Verify repository files and logs do not contain secret values.
features.notes: KeychainSecretStore is implemented and integrated with settings save, test, replace, and delete flows. API Keys are isolated by provider account suffix so DeepSeek, GLM, OpenAI, and Custom can use different secrets. The app stores only a non-secret provider setup record in UserDefaults, uses that record for Settings/onboarding/missing-key decisions, and never probes Keychain just to decide whether an API Key exists. Quick Text and Screenshot OCR translation now check the setup record before reading any API Key, and readAPIKey also checks the setup record before returning a process-cached secret. Successful saves refresh an in-memory process cache. Translation reads otherwise combine LAContext.interactionNotAllowed=true with kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail so macOS Keychain password prompts are disabled during translation. If a previous debug-build Keychain item still requires system UI, Parrot clears that provider setup record and cached secret, then shows an in-app re-enter-key/missing-key message, so retries do not keep touching Keychain. Repository scan found no token-like API secret values in tracked source. Verified ./init.sh Debug build and Scripts/keychain-cache-e2e.swift covering no-setup no-read, Settings no-read, save/cache/delete, setup-record-gates-cache, authentication-UI-fail enforcement, unsafe-record cleanup, and retry-after-cleanup no-read. 2026-07-04 adversarial verification: keychain-cache-e2e passed after updating the fake Keychain assertion for the current SDK, where LAContext.interactionNotAllowed does not read back as true even after assignment. The test now asserts that an LAContext is passed and kSecUseAuthenticationUIFail is set, preserving the non-interactive Keychain boundary. Product code still stores API Keys only in Keychain and keeps secrets out of defaults/history/docs. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity UI pass reverified privacy boundary with keychain-cache-e2e after Settings visual changes. API Keys remain Keychain-only; Settings form refactor did not add secret persistence, logs, or defaults storage.

features.description: Users can summon a lightweight text input window and quickly translate pasted or typed text.
acceptance: Open the text translation window from a global shortcut.
Focus the input field automatically.
Translate with Enter or the chosen primary action.
Close the transient window with Esc.
Copy the translation with Cmd+Enter.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Implemented QuickTextTranslationView and wired the existing Quick Text Translation menu/global shortcut entry to a real keyboard-first input window. The window auto-focuses a native NSTextView, Enter triggers translation through the configured OpenAI-compatible provider, Esc closes, Cmd+K clears, and Cmd+Enter copies the translated text and closes after a successful result. Follow-up fix changed translation from a one-shot /chat/completions request to OpenAI-compatible SSE streaming so Translation receives text deltas as they arrive; the ready message is only set after the stream finishes, and errors clear partial output and show the exception message. A repeated empty Translation-area report showed the first follow-up was incomplete: the result was still rendered by a dynamic SwiftUI ScrollView/Text inside a too-small fixed window, so the same StateObject data could appear only after a later window relayout. The result area now uses a fixed-height AppKit read-only NSTextView that is updated directly from translatedText, and the quick translation window height is increased to fit the result area. Verified ./init.sh Debug build and checked no other Swift caller still uses the old non-streaming translate path. User local E2E verification on 2026-06-22 confirmed Cmd+Shift+T opens the quick translation window, text input translates with streaming output in the Translation area, the ready message appears only after translation completes, and Cmd+Enter copies the translation and closes the window. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: Quick Text now has a clearer native header, compact language controls, distinct input/result panes, unified status banners, and a stable footer action bar; keyboard handlers and streaming translation path are unchanged. Verified by ./init.sh, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke, and ocr-source-text-editing-e2e compile/link coverage for the shared translation UI dependencies; real shortcut injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings. 2026-07-04 HTML-driven second pass: Quick Text now uses a 760x600 content target, a compact one-line language toolbar, smaller text panes, and a fixed footer action bar so the buttons are not clipped at the bottom of the window. Keyboard handlers and streaming translation behavior remain unchanged. Verified by ./init.sh, ./init.sh --run launch, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke view instantiation with target size. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity HTML pass: Quick Text moved from a compatible native layout to a closer Design/quick_text_translation/code.html structure with integrated titlebar, right-side History/Settings actions, 900x640 content, 32px content margin, title-case Input Text/Translation headers, target language menu in the Translation header, source language menu in the Input header, and fixed footer actions. Enter, Cmd+Enter, Cmd+K, Esc, streaming translation, and Keychain/privacy behavior were unchanged. Verified by ./init.sh, ./init.sh --run, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-adversarial-smoke.

features.description: The app automatically translates Chinese input to English and English input to Chinese.
acceptance: Translate Chinese source text into English.
Translate English source text into Chinese.
Preserve paragraphs, code, variable names, links, product names, and proper nouns.
Do not add explanations unless the user explicitly asks for explanation mode.
features.notes: Current implementation auto-detects target language from source text: Chinese text translates to English, otherwise text translates to Simplified Chinese. The shared translation prompt preserves paragraph structure, code, variable names, links, product names, and proper nouns, and asks the provider to output only the translation without explanations. Marked passing based on implementation review after Quick Text Translation and screenshot comparison flows were already verified with the shared streaming translation path.

features.description: Translation results are displayed in a lightweight original/translated comparison window.
acceptance: Show OCR or input text and translated text in a readable comparison layout.
Provide copy translated text and copy original text actions.
Allow retry when translation fails.
Close the result window with Esc.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Implemented screenshot OCR result as a lightweight original/translated comparison window. OCR success now auto-starts OpenAI-compatible streaming translation using the same translateStreaming path as Quick Text Translation, so translated text appears incrementally. The window shows the screenshot preview, original OCR text, translated text, copy translation, copy original, retry, and Esc/Close handling. The text panes use native AppKit read-only NSTextView wrappers to avoid first-render blank result issues seen in the quick translation window. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, and feature_list.json JSON validation. User local E2E verification on 2026-06-22 confirmed the screenshot translation comparison window works with real screenshot selection, configured provider streaming response, copy actions, retry, and Esc close. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: screenshot result, OCR failure, permission error, and selection overlay visual feedback were refined without changing local OCR, screenshot upload, or image persistence behavior. features.description: The app surfaces clear user-facing errors for permission, OCR, authentication, network, and timeout failures.
acceptance: Show missing permission guidance with next-step actions.
Show no-text-detected OCR failures without crashing.
Show authentication failures without exposing secrets.
Show network and timeout failures with retry options.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Implemented actionable user-facing errors across the P0 flows. Screenshot capture no longer stacks a Parrot error window on top of the first macOS Screen Recording permission prompt; if the user triggers screenshot capture again while Screen Recording is still not granted, the app now opens the Screenshot Capture Failed guidance window instead of silently doing nothing. OCR no-text/unavailable/failure statuses stay in the screenshot result window and include a New Screenshot action so the app does not crash or strand the user. Provider errors are mapped into concise titles, messages, and recovery suggestions; authentication messages redact token-like secrets and provider messages are truncated/sanitized before display. Quick Text Translation now has an explicit Retry button after failures, while screenshot translation keeps its Retry action for network/timeout/provider errors. Verified with ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, CGEvent smoke that Cmd+Shift+T opens Quick Text Translation, and an equivalent integration/E2E executable compiled from app sources that covered authentication redaction, network and timeout retry guidance, invalid HTTPS Base URL guidance, and blank-image OCR no-text handling. Follow-up checks added Scripts/screen-capture-access-gate-e2e.swift and verified a real Debug app with two Cmd+Shift+2 attempts: the first keeps only the system '录屏' prompt, and the second opens Parrot's Screenshot Translation permission guidance while permission is still missing. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: screenshot result, OCR failure, permission error, and selection overlay visual feedback were refined without changing local OCR, screenshot upload, or image persistence behavior. Verified by ./init.sh, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke, and ocr-source-text-editing-e2e; real screenshot shortcut injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings.

features.description: Users can view recent translation history and disable history if desired.
acceptance: Store recent text translation records when history is enabled.
Open history from the menu-bar item.
Copy previous translations.
Disable history from settings.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

Record design verification evidence in notes after UI work: screenshot/manual review, real app smoke, or documented environment limitation. Do not use /Design prototypes as the baseline unless the user explicitly requested prototype alignment.
features.notes: Implemented local text-only translation history. Successful Quick Text and Screenshot translations add records through TranslationHistoryStore when history is enabled. Records are stored as JSON under Application Support/Parrot/translation-history.json, capped to the most recent 50 entries, and never include screenshot images or API Keys. Added a menu-bar Translation History window with recent original/translation pairs, Copy Translation, Copy Original, Clear History, and Esc/Close. Added a Save translation history toggle in Settings; disabling it prevents new records from being saved while leaving existing records available until cleared. Follow-up UI fix keeps each history card at a stable summary height, truncates long original/translation previews, and adds a View Details sheet with scrollable full original and translation text so long content cannot overlap the next record. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, a source-linked E2E executable (Scripts/translation-history-e2e.swift) covering default enablement, JSON persistence/reload, disabled-save behavior, max-count trimming, clear, and NSPasteboard copy, plus real app smoke tests via ./init.sh --run and status menu AX click that opened the Translation History window. A screenshot check with long records confirmed the summary cards no longer overflow into the next record; coordinate-click detail validation was blocked by macOS Accessibility for osascript in this environment. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: history window empty, row, detail, and footer layouts now use shared native components and text-only privacy boundaries remain unchanged. Verified by /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke and /private/tmp/parrot-history-persistence-smoke; the existing translation-history-e2e still fails only on the known NSPasteboard assertion in this session. 2026-07-04 UI/adversarial verification: TranslationHistoryView instantiated at the reviewed 760x580 target size, and adversarial history persistence confirmed source/translation text records do not include screenshot image, screenRect, or base64 fields.

Persist shortcut preferences.
Detect conflicts or invalid shortcuts.
Apply changed shortcuts without restarting when practical.
Expose shortcut preferences from the unified Settings window alongside existing Model and Privacy settings.

features.notes: Implemented custom shortcuts for the two existing global actions only: Quick Text Translation and Screenshot Translation. ShortcutPreferences persists Codable keyboard descriptors in UserDefaults with defaults Cmd+Shift+T and Cmd+Shift+2. The unified Settings window now exposes Model, Shortcuts, and Privacy sections; Shortcuts includes native key recording, per-action validation, conflict detection, Restore Defaults, and Save Shortcuts. Saving valid shortcuts calls GlobalShortcutManager.reloadShortcuts() so Carbon hot keys are re-registered without restarting when the Settings window is used. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, source-linked E2E Scripts/custom-shortcuts-e2e.swift for defaults/persistence/conflicts/invalid Shift-only combinations/reset behavior, and a real App smoke test: preloaded Cmd+Option+Y for Quick Text, launched with ./init.sh --run, sent the shortcut while Finder was frontmost, and observed the Quick Text Translation window. Also verified the status menu opens the unified Settings window. The test override was removed after verification. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: shortcut rows now use native grouped panels and unified status banners while preserving recorder, conflict detection, persistence, and hot-reload flow. Verified by custom-shortcuts-e2e, ./init.sh, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke; real global shortcut event injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings. 2026-07-04 verification: custom-shortcuts-e2e passed after UI refactor, covering defaults, persistence, conflicts, invalid Shift-only shortcut rejection, restore defaults, and legacy migration. Settings sidebar now exposes Shortcuts as a native sub-section. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity UI pass reverified shortcut configuration with custom-shortcuts-e2e after Settings sidebar/titlebar refactor. Recorder, conflicts, invalid Shift-only rejection, restore defaults, and legacy migration remain intact.

features.notes: Implemented Open Settings as the third configurable GlobalShortcutAction with default Cmd+Option+,. The Shortcuts settings section now exposes Quick Text Translation, Screenshot Translation, and Open Settings with the existing recorder, persistence, conflict/invalid validation, Restore Defaults, and save-time hot-key reload path. Existing two-action ShortcutPreferences decode with the new Open Settings default so older user custom shortcuts are preserved. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, source-linked Scripts/custom-shortcuts-e2e.swift covering defaults/persistence/conflicts/invalid combinations/reset/legacy migration, and a real Debug app smoke: launched via ./init.sh --run, confirmed no Parrot windows, activated Finder, sent Cmd+Option+, via CGEvent, and observed owner=Parrot name=Settings. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: Settings and Shortcuts surfaces were polished without changing the Open Settings action or default Cmd+Option+, descriptor. Verified by custom-shortcuts-e2e, ./init.sh, and /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke; real global shortcut event injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings. 2026-07-04 verification: custom-shortcuts-e2e passed for the default Cmd+Option+, shortcut. A real CGEvent attempt to open Settings from the running Debug app was blocked by hiservices-xpcservice connection warnings in this automation session.

Support at least Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, French, and Spanish as explicit language choices.
Support one-click source-target swap and use the swapped direction for retry or retranslate.
Prevent saving or translating with the same explicit source and target language.

features.notes: Implemented TranslationLanguagePreferences with Auto source, Auto Opposite target, explicit Chinese/English/Japanese/Korean/French/Spanish choices, persistence in UserDefaults, validation that blocks same explicit source/target, and swap rules for explicit pairs, Auto -> explicit target, and Auto Opposite using the latest detected source. Quick Text Translation and Screenshot Translation result windows now show the shared language controls and pass the active preferences into OpenAICompatibleProviderClient, so the actual system prompt includes the resolved source and target language. Verified ./init.sh Debug build, source-linked E2E Scripts/translation-language-controls-e2e.swift covering defaults, persistence, prompt contents, invalid same-language rejection, and swap behavior, git diff --check, and a real Debug app smoke where Cmd+Shift+T opened the Quick Text Translation window after ./init.sh --run. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: DESIGN.md and explicit /Design prototypes were reviewed; the changed surface now uses shared native header/status/panel/footer components, semantic macOS colors, system font, native controls, and 8px-or-less rounded panels. Verified by ./init.sh Debug build/signing plus /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke; related source E2Es passed where applicable. Real CGEvent shortcut smoke was limited by hiservices-xpcservice event-delivery warnings in this automation session. 2026-07-04 HTML-driven second pass: shared language controls were compressed into a single native toolbar row used by Quick Text and Screenshot Translation, keeping source/target, swap, detected-language hint, validation, and Again behavior while reducing visual weight. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity HTML pass: language controls were moved out of the standalone toolbar and embedded into Quick Text section headers and Screenshot pane headers, preserving source/target selection, swap, validation, persistence, and retranslation while matching the prototypes' visual structure more closely.

features.notes: Implemented TranslationStyle with Accurate default plus Natural, Professional, and Concise options stored in UserDefaults. Settings now has a Translation section exposing the style segmented control and guidance that open translation windows can use Again or Retry after changing style. OpenAICompatibleProviderClient now loads the latest saved style when creating translation messages for both non-streaming and streaming translation paths, so Quick Text and Screenshot translation prompts include Translation style plus style-specific instructions without changing Provider settings or privacy behavior. Verified with source-linked E2E Scripts/translation-style-e2e.swift covering default/persistence/invalid fallback, all four prompt instructions, preservation of language/core prompt requirements, and same-text retranslation using the latest saved style. Also verified existing language prompt behavior with Scripts/translation-language-controls-e2e.swift, ran ./init.sh Debug build/signing, git diff --check, and a real Debug app smoke where Cmd+Option+, opened the Settings window while Finder was frontmost. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: DESIGN.md and explicit /Design prototypes were reviewed; the changed surface now uses shared native header/status/panel/footer components, semantic macOS colors, system font, native controls, and 8px-or-less rounded panels. features.notes: Implemented TranslationPromptPreferences with built-in default Prompt display, custom Prompt enablement, required-variable validation for {target_language} and {text}, supported variables {source_language}, {target_language}, {style}, {glossary}, and {text}, Restore Default, and invalid-custom fallback to the built-in Prompt. Settings > Translation now shows the full default Prompt, custom Prompt editor, Save Prompt, validation messages, and Restore Default. OpenAICompatibleProviderClient now loads the latest saved Prompt preferences for both non-streaming and streaming translation paths; Quick Text and Screenshot translation both already call the shared translateStreaming path, so valid custom templates affect both flows while invalid enabled templates fall back to built-in behavior. Prompt contents are not logged. Verified source-linked E2E Scripts/custom-translation-prompt-e2e.swift for default display variables, validation, persistence, active custom rendering, invalid fallback, restore default, and default saved preference loading; reran translation-style and translation-language-controls E2Es; ran ./init.sh Debug build/signing; launched the real Debug app with ./init.sh --run and verified Cmd+Option+, opens the Settings window. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: DESIGN.md and explicit /Design prototypes were reviewed; the changed surface now uses shared native header/status/panel/footer components, semantic macOS colors, system font, native controls, and 8px-or-less rounded panels. acceptance: Provide a local glossary UI in the Translation settings section.

Require source term and target term fields, with optional target language and context fields.
Prevent duplicate source terms for the same target language.
Inject only glossary entries that match the current source text into the translation Prompt.
Verify a matched glossary term changes the translation output or request Prompt while unmatched terms are not sent.

features.notes: Implemented a local personal terminology glossary in Settings > Translation. Glossary entries are stored as JSON under Application Support/Parrot/terminology-glossary.json and contain only source term, target term, optional target language, optional context, and enabled state; no API Keys or screenshot images are stored. The UI supports add, edit, delete, enable/disable, search by source or target term, required source/target validation, optional target-language scoping, optional context, and duplicate prevention for the same normalized source term plus target language. OpenAICompatibleProviderClient now loads the local glossary when building both streaming and non-streaming translation prompts, resolves the current target language, and injects only enabled entries whose source term appears in the current source text and whose target language is either Any or the resolved target language. Built-in and custom Prompt templates receive the same matched glossary text through {glossary}; unmatched entries are not sent. Verified with source-linked E2E Scripts/terminology-glossary-e2e.swift covering local JSON persistence, duplicate rejection, edit/delete/enable/disable, target-language and Any-target matching, disabled/unmatched exclusion, built-in Prompt injection, and custom Prompt {glossary} rendering. Also reran custom Prompt, translation style, and language controls E2Es, ran ./init.sh Debug build/signing, git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, and a real Debug app smoke where ./init.sh --run launched Parrot and Cmd+Option+, opened Settings as confirmed by CGWindowList. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: DESIGN.md and explicit /Design prototypes were reviewed; the changed surface now uses shared native header/status/panel/footer components, semantic macOS colors, system font, native controls, and 8px-or-less rounded panels. Verified by ./init.sh Debug build/signing plus /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke; related source E2Es passed where applicable. Real CGEvent shortcut smoke was limited by hiservices-xpcservice event-delivery warnings in this automation session.

acceptance: Show the OCR-recognized text in an editable state from the Screenshot Translation result window.
Allow users to edit the recognized text and retranslate without taking a new screenshot.
Use the edited text, not the original OCR text, for the provider request.
Save the edited source text in translation history when history is enabled.
Keep screenshot images local and do not add image persistence.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

features.notes: Implemented OCR source text editing in the Screenshot Translation result window. The Original pane now uses an editable native NSTextView initialized from the local OCR result, while the Translation pane remains read-only. Retranslate/Again uses the current edited text snapshot for language detection, OpenAICompatibleProviderClient.translateStreaming, and TranslationHistoryStore.addRecord, so history saves the actual text sent to the Provider rather than the stale OCR text. Blank edited source text disables retranslation, and if the source changes after a translation, the stale translation is cleared until the user retranslates. No screenshot image persistence was added; history remains text-only. Verified with source-linked E2E Scripts/ocr-source-text-editing-e2e.swift covering editable OCR state defaults, edited request text, custom Prompt rendering with edited text and not stale OCR text, edited source saved to local history, and no image/screenRect/base64 fields in the history JSON. Also reran translation-language-controls and custom-translation-prompt E2Es, ran ./init.sh Debug build/signing, and launched the real Debug app with ./init.sh --run. Full GUI smoke for screenshot selection/edit/retranslate/history was limited by the current automation session: Parrot was running, but injected Cmd+Shift+2 and Cmd+Shift+T events did not open windows and emitted a system hiservices-xpcservice connection warning, so the focused E2E plus Debug build were used as equivalent verification. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: screenshot result, OCR failure, permission error, and selection overlay visual feedback were refined without changing local OCR, screenshot upload, or image persistence behavior. Verified by ./init.sh, /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke, and ocr-source-text-editing-e2e; real screenshot shortcut injection was limited by hiservices-xpcservice warnings. 2026-07-04 verification: ocr-source-text-editing-e2e passed after the UI refactor. Screenshot Original remains editable, provider prompts and history use edited text, blank edited text is rejected, and no screenshot image/geometry/base64 fields are persisted. 2026-07-04 high-fidelity UI pass reverified OCR source editing with ocr-source-text-editing-e2e after Screenshot pane/header changes. Edited OCR text still drives provider prompts and history, and history JSON still excludes image, screenRect, and base64 data.

Persist the preference without changing privacy behavior or storing screenshot images.

When modifying this feature UI, verify the changed surface against DESIGN.md for native control choice, layout density, light/dark readability, loading/empty/error states, keyboard behavior, and long-text handling.

features.notes: Implemented Settings > Translation > Floating Windows with Screen Center, Mouse Nearby, and Last Position. The saved preference is stored in UserDefaults and applies to Quick Text Translation plus Screenshot Translation result/error windows. With no explicit saved override, Quick Text keeps the existing Screen Center default while Screenshot Translation results prefer the in-memory selected screenshot region and clamp back inside the visible screen when space is constrained. Last Position stores only the translation window top-left point after user movement; no screenshot images, OCR source images, API Keys, selected screenshot rectangles, or provider/prompt/history behavior were persisted or changed. Verified with source-linked E2E Scripts/floating-window-position-preferences-e2e.swift covering default/invalid preference fallback, persistence, last-position storage, screen-center/mouse-nearby/last-position frame calculation, screenshot-region-nearby placement, constrained visible-screen fallback, and no image/screenRect/base64 preference persistence. Also ran git diff --check, feature_list.json JSON validation, ./init.sh Debug build/signing, and ./init.sh --run launch. Real GUI smoke for different preferences was attempted with a temporary AppKit CGEvent/CGWindowList helper after launch, but the current automation session did not deliver Cmd+Shift+T to Parrot, so no Quick Text window was observed; the focused E2E plus Debug build/run are the acceptance evidence in this environment. 2026-07-04 UI/UX refactor verification: DESIGN.md and explicit /Design prototypes were reviewed; the changed surface now uses shared native header/status/panel/footer components, semantic macOS colors, system font, native controls, and 8px-or-less rounded panels. Verified by ./init.sh Debug build/signing plus /private/tmp/parrot-ui-view-smoke; related source E2Es passed where applicable. Real CGEvent shortcut smoke was limited by hiservices-xpcservice event-delivery warnings in this automation session.
